titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/228

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=184 status=live modified=2021-04-04 14:37:23.188203+00 | translation moa=185 status=live modified=2021-04-04 14:37:21.126698+00

p.cl: Turstanus filius leuig . vniuersis sancte matris ecclesie filiis  Salutem;

Notum sit uobis me concessisse ⁊ hac mea carta confirmasse deo ⁊ ecclesie
sancte crucis de castello puellarum ⁊ canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus ecclesiam de leuiggestun cum dimidia carucata terre ⁊ uno tofto
⁊ cum omnibus rectitudinibus ⁊ pertinentiis suis
sicut pater meus eis dedit . in liberam elemosinam ⁊ perpetuam . Volo igitur ut predicti canonici pre
nominatam ecclesiam ita libere ⁊ quiete possideant  sicut aliqua ecclesia in tota laudonia liberius ⁊ quietius possidetur.

His testibus . Gaufrido
abbate de dunferm’ . Aufrido de neob’ . Waltero monacho illius . Gaufrido de lessew’ . Petro capellano de louw’ . Waltero capellano comitis . Simundo de Ra
mesie . Rogero diacono . Dauide filio Gaufridi . Martino clerico . Waltero camerario regis de Rokesb’ . Willelmo de Aldri .
Thurstan son of Living to all sons of Holy Mother Church, Greeting:

Be it known to you that I have granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of Holyrood at Maidens’ Castle and to the canons serving God there, the church of Livingston with half a ploughgate of land and one toft and with all its rights and things that belong to it, just as my father gave to them, in free and perpetual alms. I therefore will that the aforesaid canons should possess the aforenamed church as freely and quietly as any church in the whole of Lothian is freely and quietly possessed.

With these witnesses, Geoffrey abbot of Dunfermline, Amfred of Newbattle, Walter monk of that place, Geoffrey of Lasswade, Peter the chaplain of Loquhariot, Walter the earl’s chaplain, Simon of Ramsay, Roger the deacon, David son of Geoffrey, Martin the clerk, Walter the king’s chamberlain at Roxburgh, William de Aldri.
